briefdescription.para: Picture brightness, or more precisely, the black level.
Picture color saturation or chroma gain.
Red chroma balance, as a ratio to the green channel.
Blue chroma balance, as a ratio to the green channel.

detaileddescription.para: Most devices control only digital gain with this control. Devices that recognise the difference between digital and analogue gain use VIDEO_CID_DIGITAL_GAIN and VIDEO_CID_ANALOGUE_GAIN.

briefdescription.para: Flip the image horizontally: the left side becomes the right side.
Flip the image vertically: the top side becomes the bottom side.
Frequency of the power line to compensate for.

detaileddescription.para: Avoids flicker due to artificial lighting.

briefdescription.para: Enables automatic hue control by the device.

detaileddescription.para: Setting VIDEO_CID_HUE while automatic hue control is enabled is undefined. Drivers should ignore such request.

briefdescription.para: White balance settings as a color temperature in Kelvin.

detaileddescription.para: A driver should have a minimum range of 2800 (incandescent) to 6500 (daylight).

briefdescription.para: Adjusts the sharpness filters in a camera.

detaileddescription.para: The minimum value disables the filters, higher values give a sharper picture.

briefdescription.para: Adjusts the backlight compensation in a camera.

detaileddescription.para: The minimum value disables backlight compensation.

briefdescription.para: Switch the band-stop filter of a camera sensor on or off, or specify its strength.

detaileddescription.para: Such band-stop filters can be used, for example, to filter out the fluorescent light component.
Rotate the image by a given angle, e.g. 90, 180, 270 degree.
Some devices produce data with a user-controllable alpha component. Set the value applied to the alpha channel of every pixel produced.

briefdescription.para: Camera installed on the user-facing side of a phone/tablet/laptop device.
Camera installed on the opposite side of the user.
Camera sensors not directly attached to the device or that can move freely.
Enables automatic adjustments of the exposure time and/or iris aperture.

detaileddescription.para: Manual exposure or iris changes when it is not VIDEO_EXPOSURE_MANUAL is undefined. Drivers should ignore such requests.

briefdescription.para: Determines the exposure time of the camera sensor.

detaileddescription.para: The exposure time is limited by the frame in terval. Drivers should interpret the values as 100 µs units, where the value 1 stands for 1/10000th of a second, 10000 for 1 second and 100000 for 10 seconds.

briefdescription.para: Whether the device may dynamically vary the frame rate under the effect of auto-exposure Applicable when VIDEO_CID_EXPOSURE_AUTO is set to VIDEO_EXPOSURE_AUTO or VIDEO_EXPOSURE_APERTURE_PRIORITY.

detaileddescription.para: Disabled by default: the frame rate must remain constant.

briefdescription.para: This write-only control turns the camera horizontally by the specified amount.

detaileddescription.para: The unit is undefined. A positive value moves the camera to the right (clockwise when viewed from above), a negative value to the left. A value of zero does not cause motion.

briefdescription.para: This write-only control turns the camera vertically by the specified amount.

detaileddescription.para: The unit is undefined. A positive value moves the camera up, a negative value down. A value of zero does not cause motion.

briefdescription.para: This control turns the camera horizontally to the specified position.

detaileddescription.para: Positive values move the camera to the right (clockwise when viewed from above), negative values to the left. Drivers should interpret the values as arc seconds, with valid values between -180 * 3600 and +180 * 3600 inclusive.

briefdescription.para: This control turns the camera vertically to the specified position.

detaileddescription.para: Positive values move the camera up, negative values down. Drivers should interpret the values as arc seconds, with valid values between -180 * 3600 and +180 * 3600 inclusive.

briefdescription.para: This control sets the focal point of the camera to the specified position.

detaileddescription.para: The unit is undefined. Positive values set the focus closer to the camera, negative values towards infinity.

briefdescription.para: This write-only control moves the focal point of the camera by the specified amount.

detaileddescription.para: The unit is undefined. Positive values move the focus closer to the camera, negative values towards infinity.
Manual focus adjustments while this control is on (set to 1) is undefined. Drivers should ignore such requests.

briefdescription.para: Specify the objective lens focal length as an absolute value.

detaileddescription.para: The zoom unit is driver-specific and its value should be a positive integer.

briefdescription.para: This write-only control sets the objective lens focal length relatively to the current value.

detaileddescription.para: Positive values move the zoom lens group towards the telephoto direction, negative values towards the wide-angle direction. The zoom unit is driver-specific.

Move the objective lens group at the specified speed until it reaches physical device limits or until an explicit request to stop the movement. A positive value moves the zoom lens group towards the telephoto direction. A value of zero stops the zoom lens group movement. A negative value moves the zoom lens group towards the wide-angle direction. The zoom speed unit is driver-specific.

briefdescription.para: This control sets the camera's aperture to the specified value.

detaileddescription.para: The unit is undefined. Larger values open the iris wider, smaller values close it.

briefdescription.para: This write-only control modifies the camera's aperture by the specified amount.

detaileddescription.para: The unit is undefined. Positive values open the iris one step further, negative values close it one step further.

Determines the automatic exposure compensation. It is effective only when VIDEO_CID_EXPOSURE_AUTO control is set to AUTO, SHUTTER_PRIORITY, or APERTURE_PRIORITY. It is expressed in terms of EV; drivers should interpret the values as 0.001 EV units, where the value 1000 stands for +1 EV.

Increasing the exposure compensation value is equivalent to decreasing the exposure value (EV) and will increase the amount of light at the image sensor. The camera performs the exposure compensation by adjusting absolute exposure time and/or aperture.

briefdescription.para: Enables or disables the camera's wide dynamic range feature.

detaileddescription.para: This feature allows to obtain clear images in situations where intensity of the illumination varies significantly throughout the scene, i.e. there are simultaneously very dark and very bright areas. It is most commonly realized in cameras by combining two subsequent frames with different exposure times.

briefdescription.para: This control turns the camera horizontally at the specific speed.

detaileddescription.para: The unit is undefined. A positive value moves the camera to the right (clockwise when viewed from above), a negative value to the left. A value of zero stops the motion if one is in progress and has no effect otherwise.

briefdescription.para: This control turns the camera vertically at the specified speed.

detaileddescription.para: The unit is undefined. A positive value moves the camera up, a negative value down. A value of zero stops the motion if one is in progress and has no effect otherwise.

briefdescription.para: This read-only control describes the camera position on the device It by reports where the camera camera is installed, its mounting position on the device.

detaileddescription.para: This control is particularly meaningful for devices which have a well defined orientation, such as phones, laptops and portable devices since the control is expressed as a position relative to the device's intended usage orientation. , or , are said to have the VIDEO_CAMERA_ORIENTATION_EXTERNAL orientation.

briefdescription.para: This read-only control describes the orientation of the sensor in the device.

detaileddescription.para: The value is the rotation correction in degrees in the counter-clockwise direction to be applied to the captured images once captured to memory to compensate for the camera sensor mounting rotation.

briefdescription.para: Quality (Q) factor of the JPEG algorithm, also increasing the data size.

detaileddescription.para: Analogue gain is gain affecting all colour components in the pixel matrix. The gain operation is performed in the analogue domain before A/D conversion.

briefdescription.para: Link frequency, applicable for the CSI2 based devices.
Pixel rate (pixels/second) in the device's pixel array.

detaileddescription.para: This control is read-only.

briefdescription.para: Selection of the type of test pattern to represent.

detaileddescription.para: Digital gain is the value by which all colour components are multiplied by. Typically the digital gain applied is the control value divided by e.g. 0x100, meaning that to get no digital gain the control value needs to be 0x100. The no-gain configuration is also typically the default.

sectiondef.header: Query flags, to be ORed with the control ID

detaileddescription.para: The Video control IDs (CIDs) are introduced with the same name as Linux V4L2 subsystem and under the same class. This facilitates inter-operability and debugging devices end-to-end across Linux and Zephyr.
This list is maintained compatible to the Linux kernel definitions in linux/include/uapi/linux/v4l2-controls.h
